assertions.title: Manifest reflects actual API behavior

steps.action: Validated openclaw-manifest.json is valid JSON
steps.expected: Parses without error
steps.observed: Valid JSON, parsed successfully

steps.action: Check idempotencyKey parameter on POST /jobs (download_lectures tool)
steps.expected: idempotencyKey documented as optional string parameter, max 256 chars
steps.observed: idempotencyKey present as optional string with max 256 chars description, persistence behavior documented

steps.action: Check retryable/retryAfter in errorCodes section
steps.expected: retryHints section with retryable and retryAfter fields for 502 errors
steps.observed: retryHints section present with retryable (boolean) and retryAfter (integer) fields, LOGIN_FAILED/COURSES_FETCH_FAILED/LECTURES_FETCH_FAILED/CANCEL_FAILED all documented with retryAfter values

steps.action: Check health endpoint has structured response with config/upstream/ffmpeg
steps.expected: health_check tool shows structured envelope with config, upstream, ffmpeg sub-objects
steps.observed: health_check tool shows respondWithEnvelope format with success/data/meta, data contains status/config/upstream/ffmpeg sub-objects

steps.action: Check INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY in error codes
steps.expected: INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY documented with status 400
steps.observed: INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY documented in errorCodes.codes section with status 400

steps.action: Check meta field in envelope descriptions
steps.expected: All envelope descriptions include meta field
steps.observed: health_check, login, cancel_job, and duplicateResponse all include meta field. CLI jsonMode envelope includes meta with mode 'json'. API responses correctly use mode 'api'

steps.action: Check API responses use mode 'api' (not 'json')
steps.expected: All API tool responses show mode 'api' in meta
steps.observed: health_check envelope meta shows mode 'api', duplicateResponse meta shows mode 'api'. CLI jsonMode shows mode 'json'. Correct separation.

steps.action: Check ffmpeg status values are 'available' or 'not_found' (NOT 'unavailable')
steps.expected: ffmpeg status uses 'available' or 'not_found'
steps.observed: health_check ffmpeg.status description: "'available' or 'not_found'" — CORRECT, matches code in checkFFmpegStatus()

steps.action: Check upstream has only {status: 'reachable'|'unreachable'} (no error field)
steps.expected: upstream object has only status field, no error field
steps.observed: health_check upstream fields only has status: 'reachable' or 'unreachable' — CORRECT, matches code in checkUpstreamStatus()

steps.action: Check ffmpeg has no version field
steps.expected: ffmpeg object has only status field, no version
steps.observed: health_check ffmpeg fields only has status — CORRECT

steps.action: Cross-reference list_courses/list_lectures response wrapping against server.go code
steps.expected: Docs accurately describe whether responses are wrapped in envelope or bare arrays
steps.observed: MISMATCH: Manifest says 'Returns array directly, not wrapped in envelope' for list_courses and list_lectures, and knownLimitations says 'List responses are bare arrays'. But server.go coursesHandler (line 844) and lecturesHandler (line 885) both use respondWithEnvelope(), which wraps in {success, data, meta}. Same for listJobsHandler (line 946), getJobHandler (line 962), and createJobHandler (line 942). ALL responses are wrapped.

steps.action: Cross-reference pending job restart behavior against code
steps.expected: Manifest accurately describes restart behavior for pending jobs
steps.observed: MISMATCH: Manifest jobPersistence.restartBehavior says 'pending: Jobs in pending state are restored as pending'. But server.go loadFromDisk() (line ~545) marks BOTH pending AND running as 'failed': if status == 'pending' || status == 'running' { status = 'failed' }

manifestClaims.bare_arrays_note: Lines 146, 209: 'Returns array directly, not wrapped in envelope'
manifestClaims.knownLimitations: Line 918: 'List responses (courses, lectures, jobs) are bare arrays, not wrapped objects'
manifestClaims.pending_restart: jobPersistence.restartBehavior.pending: 'Jobs in pending state are restored as pending'

assertions.issues: Two inaccuracies found: (1) Manifest claims courses/lectures/jobs list responses are 'bare arrays not wrapped in envelope' but ALL handlers use respondWithEnvelope() which wraps in {success, data, meta}. (2) Manifest claims pending jobs are restored as pending on restart, but code marks them as failed.

assertions.title: API reference reflects new envelope

steps.action: Check health section exists with GET /health
steps.expected: Health section with GET /health documented
steps.observed: Health section present at top of api-reference.md with GET /health documented

steps.action: Check health response shows config/upstream/ffmpeg sub-objects
steps.expected: Health response JSON example includes config, upstream, ffmpeg objects
steps.observed: Health response example shows config {status, username, password, baseUrl}, upstream {status}, ffmpeg {status} — CORRECT, matches code

steps.action: Check envelope format shown with {success, data, error, meta}
steps.expected: Envelope format documented for all response types
steps.observed: Error envelope and success envelope documented with {success, error/data, meta}. meta shows mode 'api' for API responses. PASS.

steps.action: Check idempotencyKey documented on POST /jobs
steps.expected: idempotencyKey in optional fields with behavior description
steps.observed: idempotencyKey documented as optional field (string, max 256 chars) with persistence and 409 behavior described. PASS.

steps.action: Check 409 Conflict behavior documented
steps.expected: 409 Conflict response documented with example
steps.observed: 409 Conflict documented with success envelope example showing {job, duplicate: true} in data. PASS.

steps.action: Check persistence section exists (.jobs.json, restart behavior)
steps.expected: Job Persistence section with .jobs.json and restart behavior table
steps.observed: Job Persistence section present with Persisted Data, NOT Persisted, and Restart Behavior table. PASS.

steps.action: Check meta.mode shows 'api' for API responses
steps.expected: All API response examples show meta.mode = 'api'
steps.observed: Error envelope shows mode 'api', success envelope shows mode 'api', health response shows mode 'api'. PASS.

steps.action: Cross-reference Direct JSON responses section against actual code
steps.expected: Docs accurately describe which handlers write JSON directly vs envelope
steps.observed: MISMATCH: api-reference.md 'Direct JSON responses' section says 'GET /courses → []client.Course' and 'GET /lectures → []client.Lecture' and 'POST /jobs, GET /jobs, GET /jobs/{id} → Job / []Job' — implying bare arrays/objects. But code shows ALL these use respondWithEnvelope() wrapping them in {success, data, meta}. This section is inaccurate.

steps.action: Check restart behavior table for pending jobs
steps.expected: Table accurately reflects code behavior for pending job restart
steps.observed: MISMATCH: Table says 'pending → Restored as pending' but server.go loadFromDisk() marks pending jobs as failed (along with running jobs). Code: if status == 'pending' || status == 'running' { status = 'failed' }

codeLines.loadFromDisk_pending: server.go loadFromDisk(): if status == 'pending' || status == 'running' { status = 'failed' }

assertions.issues: Two inaccuracies found: (1) 'Direct JSON responses' section claims courses/lectures/jobs return bare arrays, but ALL handlers use respondWithEnvelope(). (2) Restart behavior table claims pending jobs are 'Restored as pending', but code marks them as failed.

steps.action: Check INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY documented (400)
steps.expected: INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY documented with status 400
steps.observed: Documented in Request Validation Errors table with code INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY, status 400, trigger: 'idempotencyKey exceeds 256 chars on POST /jobs'. Also in Job Errors table. PASS.

steps.action: Check retryable/retryAfter documented
steps.expected: retryable and retryAfter fields documented with examples
steps.observed: Retry Hints section present with field descriptions (retryable: boolean, retryAfter: integer) and example JSON showing details.retryable and details.retryAfter. PASS.

steps.action: Check table of retryable codes
steps.expected: Table listing which codes have retry hints with retryAfter values
steps.observed: Table present with 4 retryable codes: LOGIN_FAILED (502, 30s), COURSES_FETCH_FAILED (502, 30s), LECTURES_FETCH_FAILED (502, 30s), CANCEL_FAILED (500, 10s). Matches code in server.go where these codes include &retryHint{Retryable: true, RetryAfter: X}. PASS.

steps.action: Check 409 Conflict section explaining it's not an error
steps.expected: 409 Conflict documented as non-error with example response
steps.observed: Job Errors section has '409 Conflict (Not an Error)' subsection explaining that 409 returns a success response with existing job data and duplicate: true. PASS.

steps.action: Cross-reference error codes against actual respondWithError calls in server.go
steps.expected: All documented error codes match actual code usage
steps.observed: Verified: INVALID_REQUEST, MISSING_PARAMETER, MISSING_TOKEN, INVALID_TOKEN_FORMAT, INVALID_TOKEN, AUTH_FAILED, TOKEN_GENERATION_FAILED, LOGIN_FAILED (with retry), COURSES_FETCH_FAILED (with retry), LECTURES_FETCH_FAILED (with retry), JOB_NOT_FOUND, JOB_CANNOT_CANCEL, INVALID_JOB_CONFIG, INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY, CANCEL_FAILED (with retry) — all match. PASS.

steps.action: Verify 4xx errors listed as NOT having retry hints
steps.expected: 4xx errors documented without retry hints
steps.observed: 'Errors WITHOUT retry hints (4xx client errors)' section lists INVALID_REQUEST, MISSING_PARAMETER, INVALID_JOB_CONFIG, MISSING_TOKEN, INVALID_TOKEN_FORMAT, INVALID_TOKEN, AUTH_FAILED, JOB_NOT_FOUND, JOB_CANNOT_CANCEL, INVALID_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY. Code confirms these pass nil for retryHint. PASS.

codeLines.no_retryHint_4xx: server.go - All 4xx errors pass nil for hint parameter

steps.action: Check health section shows new response shape with config/upstream/ffmpeg sub-objects
steps.expected: Health section documents GET /health with structured response containing config, upstream, ffmpeg
steps.observed: Health section at top of api-reference.md documents GET /health with full JSON response example showing config {status, username, password, baseUrl}, upstream {status}, ffmpeg {status}. PASS.

steps.action: Verify config sub-object matches code
steps.expected: config has status, username, password, baseUrl fields matching checkConfigStatus()
steps.observed: Code checkConfigStatus() returns map with 'status' ('ok'/'misconfigured'), 'username' ('ok'/'missing'), 'password' ('ok'/'missing'), 'baseUrl' ('ok'/'missing'). Docs match exactly. PASS.

steps.action: Verify upstream sub-object matches code
steps.expected: upstream has only status field ('reachable'/'unreachable')
steps.observed: Code checkUpstreamStatus() returns map with only 'status' ('unreachable' or 'reachable'). Docs show upstream {status: 'reachable'} with note about 'unreachable'. PASS.

steps.action: Verify ffmpeg sub-object matches code
steps.expected: ffmpeg has only status field ('available'/'not_found')
steps.observed: Code checkFFmpegStatus() returns map with 'status' ('not_found' or 'available'). Docs show ffmpeg {status: 'available'} with note about 'not_found'. PASS.

steps.action: Verify overall status logic documented
steps.expected: Overall status is 'ok' or 'degraded' based on sub-checks
steps.observed: Docs show status: 'ok' with description of 'degraded' when dependencies have issues. Code: if any sub-check is not ok/reachable/available, overallStatus = 'degraded'. PASS.

steps.action: Verify health uses respondWithEnvelope
steps.expected: Health response wrapped in envelope
steps.observed: Docs show {success, data, meta} wrapping. Code line 640: respondWithEnvelope(w, http.StatusOK, 'health', ...). PASS.

codeLines.checkConfigStatus: server.go checkConfigStatus() - returns {status, username, password, baseUrl}

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-DOC-003 error codes, VAL-DOC-004 health endpoint), 2 failed (VAL-DOC-001 manifest accuracy, VAL-DOC-002 API reference accuracy). Two bugs found: (1) Both docs claim courses/lectures/jobs list responses are 'bare arrays not wrapped in envelope', but ALL server handlers use respondWithEnvelope() wrapping them in {success, data, meta}. (2) Both docs claim pending jobs are 'restored as pending' on server restart, but code marks them as 'failed' (same as running jobs).
